content:
Meetings, tech debt, side projects, tools… and how to say it without guilt

5 min read
Just now

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

This image was created using an AI image creation programI used to be the ultimate “yes” person.
If someone needed a code review at 6:47 p.m., I’d say sure. If the PM wanted to squeeze in “one tiny feature” before the release, I’d stay until midnight. If a shiny new framework dropped on Twitter, I’d start a side project that same weekend.
Three years ago that “yes” attitude almost cost me my health, my marriage, and my love for coding.
Then I started saying no.
Not the rude, burned-bridge kind of no. The quiet, confident, career-preserving kind.
And everything changed.
Today I ship cleaner code, get promoted without begging, sleep like a normal human, and still have energy to play with my kids after work. Here are the exact seven things I now say “no” to without hesitation — and why every serious programmer eventually learns to do the same.
1. “Quick calls” with no agenda
I used to treat my calendar like a public park — anyone could wander in.
One Tuesday I had eight “quick syncs.” By 4 p.m. my brain was mush and I hadn’t written a single…
